priority_basis: No active frontier_research item remained in the canonical queue. R-022 left a specific unresolved gap: valid composed evidence could be accepted repeatedly because no one-time acceptance identity or replay ledger existed.
finding: A valid lifecycle composition is not one-time evidence. Final acceptance requires a unique opaque acceptance identifier checked against verifier-maintained prior acceptances.
verification: 6 deterministic Node tests passed in an isolated runtime using byte-identical pre-commit files.

claim_states.observed: The R-022 composition output has no one-time acceptance identifier or replay ledger; the new gate passed six deterministic tests.
claim_states.inferred: Reusing a previously valid output can duplicate downstream acceptance unless the verifier tracks consumption.
claim_states.proposed: Persist a bounded atomic acceptance ledger and place this gate after lifecycle composition.
claim_states.superseded: Treating valid composition as sufficient evidence of fresh, one-time acceptance.
claim_states.unresolved: Persistent storage, concurrency, multi-tab coordination, crash recovery, and retention policy remain unimplemented.

handoff: Vercel Studio should integrate the gate after lifecycle composition and implement an atomic bounded ledger. Test duplicate delivery, two-tab races, reload, bfcache restoration, and crash recovery.
